'''Delassin VI''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]]. It had [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] on its surface, and primitive [[Water/Legends|seagoing]] [[Boat/Legends|vessels]] plied the waters of the planet's [[Diniz Sea|Diniz]] and [[Zehr Sea]]s. The [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodran]] [[Xenon Nnaksta]] found work as a [[longshoreman]] at the docks servicing the Delassin VI sea vessels by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and enjoyed handling cargo there until his career path took him off-world again.

==Description==
Delassin VI was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Nnaksta" /> located in the [[Delassin system/Legends|Delassin system]], a part of the [[Calaron sector/Legends|Calaron sector]]<ref name="EAOC Delassin" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA Delassin" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC Delassin" /> Occupying the sixth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position in its [[Star system/Legends|star system]], the planet had several [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]]<ref name="CSWE Nnaksta" /> and featured the [[Diniz Sea|Diniz]] and [[Zehr Sea]]s.<ref name="Journal6" />

==History==
[[File:Xenon_Nnaksta_AJ6.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Xenon Nnaksta worked on Delassin VI for a time.]]
At some point by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="22 BBY">{{JournalCite|6|The Greel Wood Haven}} establishes that [[Xenon Nnaksta]] traveled from [[Vodran/Legends|Vodran]] to Delassin VI at least ten [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to ending his attendance of a survival school. ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', which represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one year before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]" and is therefore set in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', places Nnaksta's graduation from the [[Galactic Outdoor Survival School/Legends|Galactic Outdoor Survival School]] fourteen years prior, or in [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]]. Nnaksta must have therefore arrived on Delassin VI at some point by [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> the orphaned [[Vodran (species)/Legends|Vodran]] [[Xenon Nnaksta]] gained passage off his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]],<ref name="Journal6" /> the [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]<ref name="EAOC Vodran">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Vodran system/Legends|Vodran system]]</ref> planet [[Vodran/Legends|Vodran]], and,<ref name="Journal6" /> rejecting the traditional roles of the Vodran society,<ref name="CSWE Nnaksta" /> traveled to Delassin VI. There, the Vodran became employed as a docks [[longshoreman]] at the Diniz and Zehr Seas and enjoyed both the friendship and camaraderie of his fellow workers and the work itself, which involved the loading and unloading of cargo that often contained contraband. Eventually, Nnaksta was personally hired by the wealthy entrepreneur [[Meysen Kayson]] as a stockroom laborer for one of Kayson's retail outdoor outfitting stores. The Vodran's career path ultimately saw him travel off-world again.<ref name="Journal6" />

==Inhabitants==
Primitive [[Water/Legends|seagoing]] [[Boat/Legends|vessels]] were used on Delassin VI's Diniz and Zehr Seas. The vessels were serviced by dock workers that included [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]], [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droids]], and other beings.<ref name="Journal6" />

==Behind the scenes==
Delassin Six was first mentioned in "[[The Greel Wood Haven]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] source article authored by [[Craig Robert Carey]] and published in the [[May]] [[1995]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 6|sixth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Journal6" /> In [[2008]], ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' introduced the name "Delassin VI" for the planet.<ref name="CSWE Nnaksta" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Delassin system, and therefore Delassin VI, in grid square T-9.<ref name="TEA Delassin" />
